description: Vague complaint 'I don't feel well' resolves after agent probing

turns.user: I don't feel well
My back hurts really badly
It's about an 8 out of 10, pretty severe
It started suddenly 2 days ago
Sharp stabbing pain, worse when I bend forward
Constant, doesn't really go anywhere else

description: Pediatric: parent reports child symptoms, agent classifies correctly

turns.user: My 5-year-old has a high fever and keeps tugging at her right ear
Temperature was 102.8 this morning, ear pain started yesterday
She's really upset, crying, says it hurts a lot — maybe a 9 for her
Just the right ear, no neck pain or headache
Came on suddenly, she was fine the day before

description: Crisis: patient discloses self-harm ideation mid-conversation

turns.user: I've been feeling really depressed and hopeless for the past few weeks
I've been having thoughts of hurting myself and I don't see the point anymore
turns.note: explicit crisis language — should trigger human review

description: Emergency: chest pain + dyspnea triggers emergency escalation

turns.user: I have severe chest pain and I can't breathe properly — it started 30 minutes ago
turns.note: multiple emergency red flags in first message

description: Non-English phrasing (Spanish) — agent still extracts complaint and OPQRST

turns.user: tengo un dolor de cabeza muy fuerte
desde hace como 3 horas
como un 7 de 10
pulsante, como latidos
no se va a ningun otro lado
empeora cuando me muevo

description: Incomplete meds (no freq) → agent follow-up → frequency merged

turns.user: I take lisinopril 10mg
turns.note: med name given but no frequency — agent should ask

turns.user: Once a day every morning, I took it about 2 hours ago
No other medical history
No recent labs or tests

description: Allergy 'yes I have some' → agent asks which → names provided

turns.user: Yes I have some allergies
turns.note: vague — agent should ask which ones

turns.user: Penicillin and sulfa drugs — both give me a rash
No medications
No medical history
No recent results

description: PMH: patient lists multiple chronic conditions across two turns

turns.user: I have type 2 diabetes and high blood pressure
Oh and I had a heart attack about 4 years ago
No recent labs

description: Multi-symptom: patient lists several complaints, agent identifies primary

turns.user: I have a headache, my knee hurts, and I've been really tired lately
turns.note: three complaints — agent should pick the most prominent or ask

turns.user: The headache is the worst, I'd say a 7
Started about yesterday afternoon, came on gradually
It's throbbing, behind my eyes
It gets worse in bright light

description: Vague severity → agent gives format hint → patient clarifies with number

turns.user: My stomach hurts
It's not too bad I guess
turns.note: vague severity — agent should ask for scale

turns.user: Maybe like a 5
Started after dinner last night, crampy feeling
Lower part of my stomach, doesn't go anywhere
It comes and goes, maybe every 20 minutes
